title: WGS84 Quantum Ellipse — Geodesic Properties in QN Arithmetic
_fixture_purpose: Certifies that the geodesic properties of the WGS84 quantum ellipse (shape QN 101,9,110,119) can be expressed entirely in QN component arithmetic. Curvature ratio M/N = F/(d²-e²·s_φ). Axis ratio b/a = √F/d. Discriminant I<0 confirms ellipse. Quantum lattice points at QN-harmonic spreads coincide with geophysically significant latitudes.

witnesses.label: Equator (s_φ=0)
witnesses._note: M/N = F/d² = 12019/12100 = 0.99331. Maximum difference between M and N.

witnesses.label: 45°N (s_φ=1/2)
witnesses._note: s_φ=1/2: the spread midpoint. M/N approaches 1.

witnesses.label: Pole (s_φ=1)
witnesses._note: M/N = F/(d²-e²) = F/F = 1. At the pole, the ellipsoid is locally spherical.

quantum_lattice_points.label: Eccentricity resonance
quantum_lattice_points._note: s_φ = e²/G = 81/12181. The latitude where spread equals the eccentricity-to-blue ratio. N barely changes from equatorial value here.

quantum_lattice_points.label: Green/blue ratio (≈ Tropic)
quantum_lattice_points._note: s_φ = C/G = 1980/12181 ≈ 0.1625. Latitude ≈ 23.78° — within 0.34° of the Tropic of Cancer (23.44°). The green quadrance ratio marks a geophysically significant boundary.

quantum_lattice_points.label: 45° midpoint
quantum_lattice_points._note: s_φ = 1/2. The spread midpoint — equal sin and cos.

quantum_lattice_points.label: Red/blue ratio
quantum_lattice_points._note: s_φ = F/G = 12019/12181. High-latitude quantum point where M/N ≈ 1.

quantum_lattice_points.label: Pole
quantum_lattice_points._note: s_φ = 1. M = N = polar radius of curvature.

qa_connection.curvature_is_qn: The ratio M/N = F/(d²-e²·s_φ) expresses ellipsoidal curvature entirely in QN components {b,e,d,a,C,F,G}. No trigonometry, no transcendentals — just the quantum numbers of the Earth.
qa_connection.tropic_is_green_blue: The green/blue ratio C/G gives a quantum lattice point at 23.78° — within 0.34° of the Tropic of Cancer. The tropic (maximum sun declination = Earth's axial tilt) nearly coincides with a QN-harmonic spread. This may connect the shape QN to the axial tilt via orbital mechanics.
qa_connection.nav_stack_surface: Every cert in the navigation stack [163-167] operates ON this quantum ellipse. Dead reckoning paths curve according to M/N. Gnomonic tangent planes touch at points with curvature N. Celestial sights measure altitude from N above the reference ellipsoid.
qa_connection.orbit_independence: Shape QN (101,9,110,119) and orbit QN (59,1,60,61) share no prime factors: 101 is prime, 59,61 are twin primes, 119=7·17. By the Law of Harmonics [149], shape and orbit are structurally linked but harmonically independent.
